---
title: "3.Diabetes Burden USA Mexico Border Map"
author: "Kaustubh Wagh"
date: today
format: 
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-fold: false
    theme: cosmo
    fig-width: 12
    fig-height: 8
    warning: false
    message: false
editor: visual
---

## Map

```{r paths-config, include=FALSE}
# ── Project paths ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# All paths are resolved relative to the project root using {here}.
# Place a blank `.here` file (or an `.Rproj` file) at the repo root.

library(here)

# Shapefiles
path_shapefiles <- here("data", "external", "shapefiles")

# Processed outputs
path_proc <- here("data", "processed")

# GBD data
path_gbd <- here("data", "raw", "gbd", "GBD_diabetes_2021_USMX.rds")
```

```{r setup, warning=FALSE, echo=FALSE, include=FALSE}

# install.packages("rnaturalearth")

required_packages <- c(
  "sf", "maps", "mapdata", "usmap", "leaflet", "plotly",
  "viridis", "RColorBrewer", "scales", "tigris", "rmapshaper"
)

# Uncomment to install missing packages
install.packages(required_packages[!required_packages %in% installed.packages()])

library(sf)
library(ggplot2) 
library(dplyr)
library(viridis)
library(leaflet)
library(DT)
library(scales)
library(foreign)
library(rnaturalearth)
library(rnaturalearthhires)

library(maps)
library(mapdata)
library(usmap)
library(plotly)
library(RColorBrewer)
library(scales)
library(tigris)
library(rmapshaper)

# Set spatial options
options(tigris_use_cache = TRUE)
sf_use_s2(FALSE)  # Disable spherical geometry for simpler operations

# 1. Set path
file_path <- path_shapefiles

# 2. Load municipalities in Mexico and counties in USA

# Define US border states by their CVE_ENT codes
mx_border_states <- c(
  "02",  # Baja California
  "05",  # Coahuila
  "08",  # Chihuahua
  "19",  # Nuevo LeC3n
  "26",  # Sonora
  "28"   # Tamaulipas
)

us_border_states <- c(
  "04",  # Arizona
  "06",  # California  
  "35",  # New Mexico
  "48"   # Texas
)

mexico_mun <- st_read(file.path(file_path, "00mun.shp"), options = "ENCODING=LATIN1")
mexico_states <- st_read(file.path(file_path, "00ent.shp"), options = "ENCODING=LATIN1")

usa_counties <- st_read(file.path(file_path, "tl_2024_us_county.shp"))
usa_states <- st_read(file.path(file_path, "tl_2024_us_state.shp"))


# Rename columns in the mexico datasets
mexico_mun <- mexico_mun %>%
  rename(
    STATEFP = CVE_ENT,
    COUNTYFP = CVE_MUN,
    COUNTY_NAME = NOMGEO,
    GEOID = CVEGEO
  )

mexico_states <- mexico_states %>%
  rename(
    STATEFP = CVE_ENT,
    STATE_NAME = NOMGEO,
    GEOID = CVEGEO
  )

usa_states <- usa_states %>%
  rename(
    STATE_NAME = NAME
  )

usa_counties <- usa_counties %>%
  rename(
    COUNTY_NAME = NAME
  )

# st_geometry_type(usa_counties)
# st_geometry_type(mexico_mun)

# Standardize columns before binding
# names(mexico_mun) <- names(usa_counties)

# st_crs(usa_counties)
# st_crs(mexico_mun)

# Match mexico_mun to the CRS of usa_counties
usa_counties <- st_transform(usa_counties, st_crs(mexico_mun))
usa_states <- st_transform(usa_states, st_crs(mexico_states))


# Select only the common columns from each
usa_subset <- usa_counties %>%
  select(STATEFP, COUNTYFP, COUNTY_NAME, GEOID, geometry)%>%
  mutate(COUNTRY = "USA")

mexico_subset <- mexico_mun %>%
  select(STATEFP, COUNTYFP, COUNTY_NAME, GEOID, geometry)%>%
  mutate(COUNTRY = "Mexico")


usa_states_subset <- usa_states %>%
  select(STATEFP, STATE_NAME, GEOID, geometry) %>%
  mutate(COUNTRY = "USA")

mexico_states_subset <- mexico_states %>%
  select(STATEFP, STATE_NAME, GEOID, geometry) %>%
  mutate(COUNTRY = "Mexico")

# Append
usa_mexico_counties <- rbind(usa_subset, mexico_subset)
usa_mexico_states <- rbind(usa_states_subset, mexico_states_subset)

# Tag counties as part of US-Mexico border states
usa_mexico_counties <- usa_mexico_counties %>%
  mutate(
    is_us_border = case_when(
      COUNTRY == "USA" & STATEFP %in% us_border_states ~ "US-Mexico Border State",
      COUNTRY == "Mexico" & STATEFP %in% mx_border_states ~ "US-Mexico Border State",      TRUE ~ "Interior State"
    )
  )


excluded_statefps <- c(
  "02",  # Alaska
  "15",  # Hawaii
  "72",  # Puerto Rico
  "60",  # American Samoa
  "66",  # Guam
  "69",  # Northern Mariana Islands
  "78"   # U.S. Virgin Islands
)

# Filter counties
usa_mexico_counties_filtered <- usa_mexico_counties %>%
  filter(!(COUNTRY == "USA" & STATEFP %in% excluded_statefps))

saveRDS(usa_mexico_counties_filtered, file.path(path_proc, "map_data_counties_USMX.rds"))

# Filter states
usa_mexico_states_filtered <- usa_mexico_states %>%
  filter(!(COUNTRY == "USA" & STATEFP %in% excluded_statefps))

# Ensure STATEFP is character type
usa_mexico_counties$STATEFP <- as.character(usa_mexico_counties$STATEFP)


# Download the dataset
boundaries <- ne_download(scale = 10, type = "admin_0_boundary_lines_land", category = "cultural", returnclass = "sf")

us_mexico_border <- boundaries %>%
  filter(
    (ADM0_LEFT == "United States of America" & ADM0_RIGHT == "Mexico") |
    (ADM0_RIGHT == "United States of America" & ADM0_LEFT == "Mexico")
  )

us_mexico_border <- st_union(us_mexico_border) %>%
  st_cast("MULTILINESTRING")

# read diabetes data
diabetes_data <- readRDS(path_gbd)

# Prepare diabetes data for mapping (2021 prevalence)
diabetes_map_data <- diabetes_data %>%
  filter(
    year == 2021,
    metric_name == "Rate",
    age_name == "All ages",
    sex_name == "Both",
    cause_name == "Diabetes mellitus",
    measure_name == "Prevalence"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    # Standardize location names for matching
    location_clean = case_when(
      location_name == "Nuevo LeC3n" ~ "Nuevo Leon",
      location_name == "Nuevo LeCB3n" ~ "Nuevo Leon",
      TRUE ~ location_name
    )
  ) %>%
  rename(
    COUNTRY = country,
    STATE_NAME = location_name
  )

usa_mexico_states_filtered <- usa_mexico_states_filtered %>% 
   mutate(
    STATE_NAME = ifelse(
      tolower(trimws(STATE_NAME)) == "coahuila de zaragoza",
      "Coahuila",
      STATE_NAME
    )
  )

saveRDS(usa_mexico_states_filtered, file.path(path_proc, "map_data_states_USMX.rds"))


# join state-level diabetes data with shapefiles
# Join using both STATE_NAME and COUNTRY

usa_mexico_states_filtered <- 

joined_diabetes_map_states_data <- left_join(
  usa_mexico_states_filtered,
  diabetes_map_data,
  by = c("STATE_NAME", "COUNTRY")
)

saveRDS(joined_diabetes_map_states_data, file.path(path_proc, "GBD_diabetes_2021_map_data_states_USMX.rds"))

# For county-level diabetes data in Mexico only mortality data exist using INEGI.  
# For other country-level data exist in ENSUNAT data sets for mexico but its survey samples are weighted at state-level 
# For County-level data for USA explore BRFSS and other CDC resources liek CDC WONDER
#  Bring dataset which has been combined for USA and Mexico from file 2. county-level mortality

# load datasets
load("county_dm_mor_data_USMX.RData")
map_data_counties_USMX <-readRDS(file.path(path_proc, "map_data_counties_USMX.rds"))

# join county-level diabetes data with shapefiles
# Join using both COUNTYFP, STATEFP, COUNTRY
joined_diabetes_map_county_data <- left_join(
  map_data_counties_USMX %>% select(-COUNTY_NAME, -GEOID),
  county_dm_mor_data_USMX,
  by = c("COUNTRY","STATEFP", "COUNTYFP")
)

# str(joined_diabetes_map_county_data)
# glimpse(joined_diabetes_map_county_data)


# Save as RData file (could save multiple objects) - Recommended for R
save(joined_diabetes_map_county_data, file = "joined_diabetes_map_county_data.RData")


```

## 1. Static Choropleth Maps

#### State

```{r state_map1, warning=FALSE, echo=FALSE}

#| label: fig-static-choropleth
#| fig-cap: "Diabetes Prevalence: US-Mexico Border States Only (2021)"


# Define border states for reference
border_states <- c(
  "Arizona", "California", "New Mexico", "Texas",  # US
  "Baja California", "Chihuahua", "Coahuila", 
  "Nuevo LeC3n", "Nuevo Leon", "Sonora", "Tamaulipas"  # Mexico
)

# Create static choropleth map with proper NA handling
border_choropleth <- ggplot(joined_diabetes_map_data) +
  # All regions in gray first (interior states/regions)
  geom_sf(fill = "grey85", color = "white", size = 0.2) +
  
  # Overlay border states with diabetes data
  geom_sf(
    data = joined_diabetes_map_data %>% filter(!is.na(val), metric_name == "Rate"),
    aes(fill = val), 
    color = "white", 
    size = 0.3
  ) +
  
  scale_fill_viridis_c(
    name = "Diabetes\nPrevalence\n(per 100,000)",
    option = "plasma",
    labels = comma_format(),
    guide = guide_colorbar(
      title.position = "top",
      title.hjust = 0.5,
      barwidth = 15,
      barheight = 1.5
    )
  ) +
  
  labs(
    title = "Diabetes Prevalence: US-Mexico Border States (2021)",
    subtitle = "Age-standardized rates per 100,000 population\nGray areas = Interior states/regions (no border health data)",
    caption = "Data: IHME Global Burden of Disease 2021\nAnalysis limited to US-Mexico border states only"
  ) +
  
  theme_void() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(size = 18, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5, margin = margin(b = 5)),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 12, hjust = 0.5, color = "gray40", margin = margin(b = 10)),
    plot.caption = element_text(size = 10, hjust = 0.5, color = "gray60", margin = margin(t = 10)),
    legend.position = "bottom",
    legend.title = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 10),
    plot.margin = margin(20, 20, 20, 20),
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "white", color = NA),
    plot.background = element_rect(fill = "white", color = NA)
  )

#print(border_choropleth)

# Add labels ONLY for border states with data
border_data_only <- joined_diabetes_map_data %>%
  filter(!is.na(val),metric_name == "Rate")

if(nrow(border_data_only) > 0) {
  
  # Calculate centroids for labeling ONLY border states
  centroids <- border_data_only %>%
    st_centroid() %>%
    mutate(
      lon = st_coordinates(.)[,1],
      lat = st_coordinates(.)[,2]
    ) %>%
    st_drop_geometry()
  
  labeled_map <- border_choropleth +
    # Add border state labels with values
    geom_text(
      data = centroids,
      aes(x = lon, y = lat, label = paste0(
        substr(STATE_NAME, 1, 3), "\n", 
        round(val, 0)
      )),
      color = "white",
      fontface = "bold",
      size = 2,
      stroke = 0.3
    ) +
    
    # Add border line to emphasize US-Mexico boundary
    geom_hline(
      yintercept = 32.5,  # Approximate border latitude
      color = "#FF4444", 
      size = 1.2, 
      alpha = 0.8, 
      linetype = "dashed"
    ) +
    
    labs(
      title = "Diabetes Prevalence: US-Mexico Border States (2021)",
      subtitle = "Colored regions = Border states with data | Gray regions = Interior states/regions\nRed dashed line indicates approximate US-Mexico border",
      caption = "Data: IHME Global Burden of Disease 2021 | Analysis limited to border states only"
    )
  
  print(labeled_map)
  
} else {
  cat("No border state data found for mapping. Check data matching.\n")
}

```

#### County

```{r county_map1, warning=FALSE, echo=FALSE}

# Define border states for reference
border_states <- c(
  "Arizona", "California", "New Mexico", "Texas",  # US
  "Baja California", "Chihuahua", "Coahuila", 
  "Nuevo LeC3n", "Nuevo Leon", "Sonora", "Tamaulipas"  # Mexico
)

# Create static choropleth map with proper NA handling
border_county_choropleth <- ggplot(joined_diabetes_map_county_data) +
  # All regions in gray first (interior states/regions)
  geom_sf(fill = "grey85", color = "white", size = 0.2) +
  
  # Overlay border states with diabetes data
  geom_sf(
    data = joined_diabetes_map_county_data %>% filter(!is.na(`Crude Rate`)),
    aes(fill = `Crude Rate`), 
    color = "white", 
    size = 0.3
  ) +
  
  scale_fill_viridis_c(
    name = "Diabetes\nCrude Death Rate\n(per 100,000)",
    option = "plasma",
    labels = comma_format(),
    guide = guide_colorbar(
      title.position = "top",
      title.hjust = 0.5,
      barwidth = 15,
      barheight = 1.5
    )
  ) +
  
  labs(
    title = "Diabetes Mortality: US-Mexico Border States (2021)",
    subtitle = "Age-standardized rates per 100,000 population\nGray areas = Interior states/regions (no border health data)",
    caption = "Data: IHME Global Burden of Disease 2021\nAnalysis limited to US-Mexico border states only"
  ) +
  
  theme_void() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(size = 18, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5, margin = margin(b = 5)),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 12, hjust = 0.5, color = "gray40", margin = margin(b = 10)),
    plot.caption = element_text(size = 10, hjust = 0.5, color = "gray60", margin = margin(t = 10)),
    legend.position = "bottom",
    legend.title = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 10),
    plot.margin = margin(20, 20, 20, 20),
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "white", color = NA),
    plot.background = element_rect(fill = "white", color = NA)
  )

# print(border_county_choropleth)

# Add labels ONLY for border states with data
border_data_only <- joined_diabetes_map_county_data %>%
  filter(!is.na(`Crude Rate`))

if(nrow(border_data_only) > 0) {
  
  # Calculate centroids for labeling ONLY border states
  centroids <- border_data_only %>%
    st_centroid() %>%
    mutate(
      lon = st_coordinates(.)[,1],
      lat = st_coordinates(.)[,2]
    ) %>%
    st_drop_geometry()
  
  labeled_map <- border_county_choropleth +
    # Add border state labels with values
    geom_text(
      data = centroids,
      aes(x = lon, y = lat, label = paste0(
        substr(STATE_NAME, 1, 3), "\n", 
        round(`Crude Rate`, 0)
      )),
      color = "white",
      fontface = "bold",
      size = 2,
      stroke = 0.3
    ) +
    
    # Add border line to emphasize US-Mexico boundary
    geom_hline(
      yintercept = 32.5,  # Approximate border latitude
      color = "#FF4444", 
      size = 1.2, 
      alpha = 0.8, 
      linetype = "dashed"
    ) +
    
    labs(
      title = "Diabetes Mortality: US-Mexico Border States (2021)",
      subtitle = "Colored regions = Border states with data | Gray regions = Interior states/regions\nRed dashed line indicates approximate US-Mexico border",
      caption = "Data: IHME Global Burden of Disease 2021 | Analysis limited to border states only"
    )
  
  print(labeled_map)
  
} else {
  cat("No border state data found for mapping. Check data matching.\n")
}

```

```{r map-test, warning=FALSE, include=FALSE}
# Plot
us_mx_border_map <- ggplot() +
  geom_sf(data = usa_mexico_counties_filtered, 
          aes(fill = is_us_border), 
          color = "white", 
          size = 0.05) +
  geom_sf(data = usa_mexico_states_filtered, 
          fill = NA, 
          color = "#2C3E50", 
          size = 0.6) +
   geom_sf(data = us_mexico_border, 
          color = "red", 
          size = 20,    # Thicker line for international border
          linetype = "solid") +
  scale_fill_manual(
    name = "State Type",
    values = c("US-Mexico Border State" = "#3498DB", "Interior State" = "#ECEFF1"),
    guide = guide_legend(override.aes = list(color = "black", size = 0.5))
  ) +
  theme_void() +
  labs(
    title = "Mexicob
```
